channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration: U of M student in ICE custody should remain jailed
item.description: The Trump administration says a University of Minnesota graduate student from Turkey should remain jailed while the Department of Homeland Security moves forward with removal proceedings. That's according to court documents filed just ahead of a deadline late Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Here's the list of books the Naval Academy removed from its library during DEI purge
item.description: Included in the purge is “How to be Anti-Racist" by Ibram X. Kendi, as well as “I Know Why The Caged Bird Sings" by American writer and poet Maya Angelou.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks order requiring Trump administration to reinstate thousands of federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday blocked an order for the Trump administration to return to work thousands of federal employees who were let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department will narrow its focus on crypto-related enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is disbanding a team of prosecutors who targeted cryptocurrency crimes and is shifting its focus away from complex crypto-related cases involving banking and securities law.
item.dc:creator: Alan Suderman And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Navy chief 'regrets' costly missile strikes against Houthis, pushes for cheaper Red Sea defense
item.description: Adm. James Kilby says expensive Red Sea missile defenses are not sustainable and calls on the military-industrial base to speed up cheaper munitions production.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is expected to sign executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign executive orders aimed at boosting coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Boasberg contempt showdown looms after Supreme Court hands Trump immigration win
item.description: President Donald Trump praised the Supreme Court’s ruling, but a contempt hearing Tuesday could upend the win as Judge James Boasberg presses officials on secretive deportations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs about renegotiating trade deals, not jobs: Kasparian
item.description: Kasparian doesn't think Trump's tariffs are aimed at bringing factory jobs back despite what the president has said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration fires senior Navy female officer at NATO. She appeared on a 'woke' list
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. Navy Vice Adm. Shoshana Chatfield, the only woman on NATO's military committee, was fired over the weekend by the Trump administration, U.S. officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First Black Republican woman in Congress honored in Utah after her death from brain cancer
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Family and friends of former U.S. Rep. Mia Love gathered Monday in Salt Lake City to honor the life and legacy of the first Black Republican woman elected to Congress after she died of brain cancer last month at age 49.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts has agreed to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens more tariffs on China as global markets plunge
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering additional tariffs on China. The Republican president's threat came Monday after Beijing retaliated against his announcement of tariffs last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Freedom of speech is shifting under the Trump administration. We’re exploring how
item.description: Many Americans worry freedom of speech is fading, while others feel empowered to say what they want. NPR's Morning Edition explores this dynamic in a new series, The State of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel, Arezou Rezvani, Taylor Haney, and Kyle Mackie, MPR News

item.title: How does deportation work, and how much does it cost? We break it down
item.description: NPR spoke to immigration experts and lawyers and reviewed government documents to break down the steps and associated costs of the U.S. deportation process.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Alyson Hurt, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he hopes Sununu runs for Senate in New Hampshire
item.description: "He came to my office, came to the Oval Office, and met with Chris Sununu, and I support him fully. I hope he runs," Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit. But now they are faced with losing the biggest export market for many of their crops after China retaliated against President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna clashes with GOP House leaders, plans tele-town hall
item.description: The Pinellas County Republican is pushing to allow new parents the ability to vote by proxy, or remotely, for up to 12 weeks after having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No bathroom breaks: How Booker accomplished record Senate speech
item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) set a new record for the longest Senate speech in history on Monday and Tuesday by holding the upper chamber’s floor for more than 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California lawmakers reject bills to ban trans athletes' participation in girls sports
item.description: California lawmakers won't change state policies allowing transgender kids and teens to play on sports teams consistent with their gender identities.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, Fox 5
